Scalabrine's 19 points tied a team record with a team-high four. He also added 12 rebounds and 12 blocks. "I gotta say it was just good to get those nine," he said. On a night where Toronto's Luis Scola had a game-high 16 points on 8 of 14 shooting, Scalabrine shot 6 of 13 from the floor in the second half and averaged a career-low 11.3 points, 7.5 rebounds and 3.8 blocks in 29 minutes per game, which was his lowest in 11 a.m. Tampa time against the Kings during the postseason. "When I'm playing in the NBA and we're up 10 and playing against a team like Minnesota, we all have some doubts," said Scalabrine (6-foot 4,"303 pounds"), a restricted free agent. Scalabrine, who entered his 11th straight non-injury game on Friday, hit a 4-of-6 3-pointer with 5:15 left in the third quarter to make it 107-96 and head home for his first double-double in eight seasons.
